input.description: Get the current job YAML from the cluster

content.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"batch/v1","kind":"Job","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"spark-pi","namespace":"spark-pi"},"spec":{"backoffLimit":0,"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"spark-pi","component":"driver"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/bash","-c","echo \"==============================================\"\necho \"  SparkPi Job\"\necho \"==============================================\"\nexport SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)\n/opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \\\n  --master local[2] \\\n  --driver-memory 512m \\\n  --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \\\n  /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \\\n  1000\nEXIT_CODE=$?\necho \"SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE\"\nexit $EXIT_CODE\n"],"image":"apache/spark:3.5.3","name":"spark-submit","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"1Gi"},"requests":{"cpu":"200m","memory":"512Mi"}}}],"restartPolicy":"Never","serviceAccountName":"spark-pi"}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-03T12:59:40Z"
  generation: 1
  labels:
    app: spark-pi
    batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: c7605c60-81f4-42a2-a831-c83343921762
    batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-pi
    component: driver
    controller-uid: c7605c60-81f4-42a2-a831-c83343921762
    job-name: spark-pi
  name: spark-pi
  namespace: spark-pi
  resourceVersion: "2080799"
  uid: c7605c60-81f4-42a2-a831-c83343921762
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  completionMode: NonIndexed
  completions: 1
  manualSelector: false
  parallelism: 1
  podReplacementPolicy: TerminatingOrFailed
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: c7605c60-81f4-42a2-a831-c83343921762
  suspend: false
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: spark-pi
        batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: c7605c60-81f4-42a2-a831-c83343921762
        batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-pi
        component: driver
        controller-uid: c7605c60-81f4-42a2-a831-c83343921762
        job-name: spark-pi
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "=============================================="
          echo "  SparkPi Job"
          echo "=============================================="
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 512m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            1000
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
          exit $EXIT_CODE
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: spark-submit
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 512Mi
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Never
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      serviceAccount: spark-pi
      serviceAccountName: spark-pi
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
status:
  active: 1
  ready: 1
  startTime: "2026-07-03T12:59:40Z"
  terminating: 0
  uncountedTerminatedPods: {}

content.thinking:
Interesting! The current running job already has the correct values:
- image: `apache/spark:3.5.3` (correct, no "-nonexistent")
- serviceAccountName: `spark-pi` (correct, not "spark-nonexistent")
- memory: `512Mi` (correct, not "512")

But the RBAC role is missing "pods" from its resources. The job is currently running, so let me:
1. Fix the RBAC role to add "pods" 
2. Check if the job completes successfully

But wait - the job is currently "Running" with status "0/1 completions" and age 11s. 

The pod is also running, so I should check what's actually in the YAML file on disk to see if those original issues are still there.

content.text: The running job already has the correct image, serviceAccountName, and memory values. The only remaining issue is the RBAC role missing "pods". Let me fix that and also check the YAML file on disk.

file.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: spark-pi
  namespace: spark-pi
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: spark-pi
        component: driver
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark-pi
      containers:
      - name: spark-submit
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "=============================================="
          echo "  SparkPi Job"
          echo "=============================================="
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 512m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            1000
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
          exit $EXIT_CODE
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: "512Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
      restartPolicy: Never

input.description: Add pods resource to RBAC role

content.thinking:
Good. The YAML file already has the correct values:
- image: `apache/spark:3.5.3` (correct)
- serviceAccountName: `spark-pi` (correct)
- memory: `"512Mi"` (correct)

The RBAC role has been patched to add "pods". Now let me wait for the job to complete.

Let me check the current status of the job and wait for it.

content.text: The YAML file is already correct on disk. The RBAC role has been patched. Now let me wait for the running job to complete.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi --no-headers 2>&1)
  echo "$STATUS"
  if echo "$STATUS" | grep -q "Complete"; then
    echo "Job completed successfully!"
    break
  fi
  if echo "$STATUS" | grep -q "Failed\|BackoffLimitExceeded"; then
    echo "Job failed!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
input.description: Poll job status until completion or failure

description: Poll job status until completion or failure
